title: Web Data, Fetch, Storage, and Security Boundaries · Runtime Trace: Origin Permissions
summary: Load and cache public service data while handling hostile input, offline states, privacy, and response failures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js origin permissions increment in a resilient privacy-aware service-data client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries.
Keep untrusted data in text or validated structured sinks and avoid dynamic code or unsafe markup construction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
